shared_properties: Hazard tile aligns to a 16x16 grid and keeps a 1 px dark outline so contact edges remain readable at speed.
Core palette metadata remains primary #3B3347, background #0D0D0F, and accent #E07B39 while local hazard colors stay inside specs.colors_hex.
Occupied pixels stay concentrated in the lower 14 px so the point tips remain visually dangerous against dark floors.
differences: Three spike tips rise to y2, y0, and y2 respectively for a centered 3-prong silhouette.
Rust-red metal fill occupies most of the body while cold highlights cover fewer than 12 occupied pixels.
No animation is present so the tile communicates instant damage with a stable silhouette.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the 16x16 spike hazard, one-hit HP loss, and dungeon tile scale used throughout the descent route.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the rusted, hostile dungeon material language so the spike tile sits naturally inside the shaft mood.

reference_images.label: player_concept
reference_images.purpose: Grounds player scale against the spike tile so one contact threat feels fair at 16x24 versus 16x16 proportions.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle for static hazards or SpriteSheetBundle for animated hazards, all rendered with nearest-neighbor sampling.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load hazard textures and atlases during the shared Loading state and expose handles through HazardVisualRegistry keyed by asset_key.
runtime_notes: Keep collision volumes in gameplay components rather than inferring them from visual pixels.
Align every hazard origin to the 16x16 grid unless the authored size is 24x24 or 48x16 by design.
Use visibility toggles or atlas index changes instead of runtime texture swaps to avoid hitching during repeated descent loops.

context.usage: Stationary damage tile placed on floors, ceilings, or wall cut-ins.

silhouette_notes: Center prong reaches the top row at y0.
Base occupies y11-y15 with a continuous 16 px footing strip.
Prong gaps remain at least 1 px wide for readability at 4x scale.

acceptance_criteria: Hazard tile is exactly 16x16 px with the tallest prong touching y0 and the base touching y15.
At least 70% of occupied pixels use #8B2020 or #3B3347 so the spike reads as rusted metal rather than stone.
No frame-based animation, anti-aliasing, or background fill is present.
